title: Ultimate merchandise Evangelion Pachinko machines
wikitext:
[]: Ultimate merchandise - Evangelion Pachinko machines             Quote Post by custompachinko » Tue Feb 23, 2010 9:39 pm I just got the two latest Evangelion pachinko machines sent directly from Japan. The last one was released into parlors last April and these are some of the first ones to make it to American soil (be jealous)!!!! I've got some video online at [url]www.custompachinko.com/pachinko.html[/url] and i'm taking some more now - I just hit a jackpot on Eva 5. If anybody wants to know more about the machines let me know - I'd be glad to give some more info!

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Tue Feb 23, 2010 10:26 pm I don't suppose they give away Tunniel's real name anywhere, do they?

[]:             Quote Post by custompachinko » Tue Feb 23, 2010 10:35 pm haven't seen it... but maybe if i keep playing?

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Thu Feb 25, 2010 9:47 pm I have seen the videos on the website, but am very confused on how it works. Is it complex?

[]:             Quote Post by custompachinko » Thu Feb 25, 2010 9:54 pm It's really easy. All you have to do is put balls in and turn the knob. That determines how strong the balls will be shot out. If you get a ball in the center then the wheels spin - three of the same number in a row means you go into jackpot mode. That's the really simplified version. There are differences between the jackpots and lots of different modes and songs, but to get an idea of whats going on that's all you need. Do you need an explanation about any particular video or just about pachinko in general?

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Fri Feb 26, 2010 10:13 am I HAVE GOT TO PLAY THOSE PACHINKO MACHINES ONE DAY. They say that once you play'em, you can't stop playing... unless it's by force, of course.

[]:             Quote Post by custompachinko » Fri Feb 26, 2010 11:31 am Well if anyone is thinking of buying one I'd be more than happy to let them try them out!

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Fri Feb 26, 2010 12:27 pm Is international shipping free too? How's the customs paperwork? What are the dimensions of these machines?

[]:             Quote Post by custompachinko » Thu Oct 27, 2011 9:40 am AchtungAffen wrote: Is international shipping free too? How's the customs paperwork? What are the dimensions of these machines?

[]:             Quote Post by Hawq » Fri Dec 23, 2011 2:45 pm once again becomes time to pray for a lottery win to be able to obtain such wonderous devices

[]:             Quote Post by custompachinko » Sat Dec 24, 2011 9:56 am We still have two left that are signed by Tiffany Grant and if anyone is interested let me know!

[]:             Quote Post by Kasowaree » Tue Jan 03, 2012 2:26 pm If you still have either of the signed ones I'm more then willing to buy one.

[]:             Quote Post by custompachinko » Tue Jan 03, 2012 4:03 pm We have sold out of the Eva ones, but still have a Nadesico slot and pachinko machine that are signed.
